methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series D → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.5x) to $3.0B last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 5 same-sector + same-stage peers in AI & Software. Range: $1.0B - $8.6B.

data.narrative:
ElevenLabs, a Series D–stage AI & Software company, is evaluated against a comp universe of five same-sector, same-stage private peers (Wayve, Cohere, Hugging Face, Hive AI, Labelbox) and one public comparable (Palantir). The target's last post-money valuation stands at $3.0B on $500M raised to date.

Two triangulation methods are applicable. Stage-progression analysis applies a 1.5x step-up multiplier typical of Series D advancement to the $3.0B base, yielding $4.5B. Peer-median analysis across the five comparables—which range from $1.0B (Labelbox) to $8.6B (Wayve)—produces a median of $4.5B, consistent with the stage-progression result. Both methods converge on $4.5B, reducing valuation dispersion.

The analysis supports a triangulated range of $3.8B to $5.2B, centered at $4.5B. Key uncertainties include the wide peer distribution ($1.0B–$8.6B), reflecting heterogeneous product positioning and revenue maturity within the AI sector at this stage. The current $3.0B+ valuation implies limited step-up from Series C entry, suggesting either market repricing of AI infrastructure risk or earlier-stage pricing discipline. Near-term catalysts—revenue scale, customer concentration, burn rate—will clarify positioning within the derived range.
